pre_conversation.line: The gods are always watching, tread carefully.
Stay close, danger lurks around every corner.
The old tales speak of this place, but they never mentioned how cold it is.
The woods whisper of ancient magics.
I've heard stories of a mighty warrior with a boy by his side.
The winds carry a scent of battle. We should be ready.
The trees here have seen more than we ever will.
Keep your wits about you; the creatures here are not to be trifled with.
I’ve seen that symbol before... it means death.
The world has changed since the gods began their war.
These lands are unforgiving, but so are we.
There’s a storm brewing... something doesn’t feel right.
The cold bites deeper than any sword.
We should move quickly; I don’t trust the silence.
The runes speak of a prophecy, one that’s yet to unfold.
The gods demand sacrifice, but we shall give them none.
The ravens are watching us, always watching.
There’s blood in the air. The gods are angry.
A warrior’s path is never without pain.
They say the gods have abandoned this realm.
These old stones have witnessed countless battles.
There’s something ancient about this place... something powerful.
The spirits of the fallen linger here, restless.
The air is thick with magic; I can feel it in my bones.
Beware the light, for it can deceive as much as the dark.
The mountains hold many secrets, most of them deadly.
The gods play their games, but we are not their pawns.
The world serpent stirs... something must be amiss.
We are but mortals, yet the gods fear us.
Every step here feels like a step closer to the end.
